questions.question: What is India’s share in global paper production?
questions.explanation: India accounts for approximately 5% of worldwide paper production as per recent industry data. India is the 15th largest paper producer globally by volume. The Indian paper industry comprises more than 800 mills. India’s total annual paper production exceeds 20 million tonnes in recent years.

questions.question: When was the first machine-made paper introduced in India?
questions.explanation: The first machine-made paper was introduced in 1812. India's newspaper and publication industry was the main consumer of the machine-made paper.

questions.question: Which is a principal raw material for paper manufacturing in India?
questions.explanation: India's paper industry uses wood pulp, bamboo, and agricultural waste as principal raw materials. Bamboo has been used since 1921 in Indian mills. Bagasse from sugarcane and wheat straw are significant agro-residues. Use of recycled fibers is also increasing. These multiple raw materials address resource limitations and support the annual production of over 20 million tonnes of paper in India.

questions.question: Which among the following is a major raw material of paper manufacturing in India?
questions.explanation: Bamboo is the major raw material for the paper industry of India. Softwood generally grows in a temperate regions and is in short supply for India. So Bamboo is widely used in India.

questions.question: Which among the following is / are raw material(s) used for paper Industry in India? Bamboo Recycled fibre Soft wood Rice husk Choose the correct option from the choices given below:
questions.explanation: The following is the major raw material used for paper Industry in India:1. Bamboo2. Recycled fibre3. Softwood4. Rice husk5. Wheat Straw6. Bagasse
